name: PY Ta 641 (the tripod tablet)
translations: two tripods of Cretan workmanship (illustrative excerpt; the tablet that confirmed Ventris's decipherment)

name: PY Er 312 (the king's estate)
translations: the king's domain; so much seed-grain: GRA 30 (illustrative excerpt)

name: KN Ga 675
translations: for the king.dat-instr.sg: [coriander?] seed - 10 units(?) (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. c. 1330 BC BCE (LM IIIA2 - LM IIIB), Knossos Ga(1) 675)

name: KN Np 267
translations: (please add an English translation of this quotation) (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. KN Np(1) 267)

name: KN Np 272
translations: (please add an English translation of this quotation) (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. KN Np(1) 272 + 7419 + fr.)

name: KN Np 85
translations: (please add an English translation of this quotation) (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. KN Np(1) 85 + 5047 + 7938 + 8057)

name: MY Ge 602
translations: mint, two bunches (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. MY Ge 602, line 5b)

name: MY Ge 605
translations: mint, one bunch (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. MY Ge 605, line 6b)

name: MY Ge 606
translations: fennel seeds: 1 khoinix ; sesame seeds: 4 khoinikes (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. c. 1300 BCE (LH IIIB1), Mycenae Ge 606)

name: PY Cn 3
translations:
They send(?) from Messene,
 to inspector [Di-wi-je-we], bulls.acc.pl (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. c. 1200 BCE (LH IIIB2 - LH IIIC), Pylos Cn(2) 3)

name: PY Eb 846
translations: Aethiops, and he holds the lease of a communal plot (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. PY Eb 846.A)

name: PY En 74
translations: now this is how the tenants hold plots belonging to Aethiops (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. PY En 74.11–12)

name: PY Eo 247
translations:
Ipasanati, servant of the god, and she holds a lease from Aethiops: 24 l. wheat
[... etc.] (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. PY Eo 247.1–7)

name: PY Fr 1202
translations: in the month of new wine for the divine mother: sage-scented oil 160 l (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. LH IIIB2/LH IIICI (c. 1250 - 1050 BCE), PY Fr 1202)

name: PY Fr 1224
translations: in the month of pa-ki-ja-ni-jo, to Poseidon, sage- and e-ti-perfumed wild-olive oil 0.8 l (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. PY Fr 1224.b)

name: PY Jn 829
translations: bronze for ships and the points for arrows and spears (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. PY Jn 829.3)

name: PY Sa 794
translations: one pair of wheels bound with bronze, unfit for service (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. PY Sa 794)

name: PY Un 267
translations:
Thus has A(r/l)xo(i)tas supplied
Thuestes the unguent-boiler:
 spices, for unguent.dat-instr.sg
 to be boiled.dat-instr.sg (illustrative excerpt; quotation via Wiktionary, ref. c. 1200 BCE (LH IIIB2/LH IIIC), Pylos Un 267)
